Title: Xi set to welcome Trump from a position of strength
Subhead: The US president is due to visit China from Wednesday, at a time when he is embroiled in the conflict with Tehran, of which Beijing is a partner. Meanwhile, the oil and gas crisis linked to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has spared China, which had taken steps to protect itself from this type of risk.

Title: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot: 'In Africa, as elsewhere in the world, Russia has been largely defeated'
Iran says US must accept its peace plan or face 'failure'
Assemblée Nationale inquiry commission exposes deep flaws in France's museum security model
Subhead: A report released on May 13 calls for increased resources to secure museums as well as a complete overhaul of the museum leadership appointment process.

Title: In Nairobi, France makes a push to invest in Africa
Subhead: Paris declared its ambition to invest in the continent at the Africa Forward Summit, which opened on May 11 in the heart of the Kenyan capital. Competition, however, is fierce across these dynamic markets.

Title: Stars descend on the carpet as Cannes Film Festival 2026 opens without Hollywood backing
Subhead: As usual in the build-up to the festival, off-screen events have dominated conversation, from the impact of AI on jobs, Hollywood's decision to ghost the event and the under-representation of women directors.

Title: The shattered Iranian lives of Manije, Mohammad-Reza and Soroush, all killed by bombings
Subhead: Before the Israeli-US bombings began, they were living as retirees and artists in different parts of Tehran. Until they met their deaths in their own homes. Tormented by distress, their loved ones abroad and in Iran recount their last conversations before fate took a tragic turn.

Subhead: The actress, who rose to fame in France in 2017, paid tribute to her primary school teacher, who inspired her passion for theater.

Title: French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
Subhead: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

Title: Cannes Film Festival sees last artists walk the red carpet before the 2027 presidential election
Subhead: The 79th edition of the film festival, opening on Tuesday, May 12, is set to be a highly political event in the city, as it is by David Lisnard, a declared presidential candidate who advocates for a right-wing primary including the far right.
